ogTitle: Drummoyne vs Dry Plain: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Drummoyne and Dry Plain.
heading: Drummoyne vs Dry Plain — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Drummoyne, New South Wales and Dry Plain, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Drummoyne has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Dry Plain (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Drummoyne or Dry Plain safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Drummoyne has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Dry Plain scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Drummoyne scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Dry Plain scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Drummoyne or Dry Plain?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Drummoyne has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
